i_formation.name: Power Run
i_formation.description: FB lead block through the A-gap. The signature I-Formation play — pure brute force.

i_formation.name: Outside Run
i_formation.description: RB sweeps the edge behind a pulling guard. Works when the defense loads the middle.

i_formation.name: Play Action Pass
i_formation.description: Fake the handoff, attack down the field. Safeties that cheat for the run get burned.

i_formation.name: Screen Pass
i_formation.description: Quick dump-off to the RB with blockers set up in front. A great blitz beater.

shotgun_trips.name: Quick Pass
shotgun_trips.description: 3-step drop, ball out before the rush arrives. Neutralizes any blitz.

shotgun_trips.name: Deep Shot
shotgun_trips.description: Vertical routes attacking single-covered WRs. High risk, high reward.

shotgun_trips.name: Draw Run
shotgun_trips.description: RB run disguised as a pass set-up. Punishes linebackers who drop into coverage.

shotgun_trips.name: RPO
shotgun_trips.description: QB reads a defender post-snap and decides run or pass. Puts the defense in a dilemma.

singleback.name: Inside Zone
singleback.description: RB reads the line and cuts to the first open gap. Hard to key on pre-snap.

singleback.name: Outside Run
singleback.description: RB sweeps the edge. Needs the corner sealed — a big gain or a loss.

singleback.name: Crossing Routes
singleback.description: WRs cross underneath. Destroys man coverage — defenders run into each other.

singleback.name: Play Action Pass
singleback.description: Fake handoff, throw deep. More effective after the run game is established.

goal_line_off.name: FB Lead
goal_line_off.description: FB dives first, RB follows the path. Maximum push at the point of attack.

goal_line_off.name: QB Sneak
goal_line_off.description: QB sneaks over the center. Highest short-yardage conversion rate in the game.

goal_line_off.name: Power Sweep
goal_line_off.description: RB runs wide behind a pulling TE. A big gain if the edge is sealed; a loss if it isn't.

goal_line_off.name: Play-Action Fade
goal_line_off.description: Fake the power run, throw back-shoulder fade to the corner. Catches the defense flat-footed.

four_three.name: Cover 2
four_three.description: Two safeties split the deep field. CBs play underneath. Limits big plays.

four_three.name: Cover 3
four_three.description: Three defenders split the deep thirds, one safety helps run. A sound all-around call.

four_three.name: MLB Blitz
four_three.description: Middle linebacker shoots the A-gap. If it works, the play is dead before it starts.

four_three.name: Man Press
four_three.description: CBs jam receivers at the line. Disrupts timing — but one beatdown is a big play.

nickel.name: Cover 2
nickel.description: Five DBs in zone. Built for passing downs — the extra coverage takes away checkdowns.

nickel.name: Cover 1 Robber
nickel.description: Man coverage outside, safety roams underneath to poach short routes.

nickel.name: Slot Blitz
nickel.description: Nickelback blitzes from the slot. The offense rarely IDs this pre-snap.

nickel.name: Zone Drop
nickel.description: All five DBs drop into coverage. Takes away every passing lane but gives up the run.

stacked_box.name: All-Out Blitz
stacked_box.description: Eight defenders converge on the line. Ball must be out in under two seconds or the QB is buried.

stacked_box.name: Cover 1 Rob
stacked_box.description: Man coverage everywhere with one deep safety as a roaming center fielder.

stacked_box.name: Read Blitz
stacked_box.description: LBs read the snap and delay-blitz — harder to pick up in protection than a fire blitz.

stacked_box.name: Spy + Contain
stacked_box.description: One LB spies the QB, OLBs crash the edges. Neutralizes scramble threats.

goal_line_def.name: Gap Fill
goal_line_def.description: Five DL each own a gap. Nothing should get through on the ground.

goal_line_def.name: Slant & Stunts
goal_line_def.description: DL slants one direction, creates confusion in OL blocking assignments.

goal_line_def.name: Goal Line Blitz
goal_line_def.description: LB shoots a gap on the snap. A single back can't account for six rushers.

goal_line_def.name: Man Press
goal_line_def.description: Physical press on the lone WR. Jams the route before it can develop.
